summary: An audit judges whether an artifact lies — not whether the future it describes is built — and every finding is carried to a documented disposition, with the verifiers themselves held to the same no-lying standard.

rules.rule: Fail or revert a sealed artifact because it describes architecture not yet built — unimplemented future design is a PASS (category 4), an improvement decision, not a fault; only false present-claims and canon contradictions are FAILs.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Every audit delivery carries a Disposition section with one row per critical finding, appended to the same delivery artifact — nothing stays 'acknowledged but idle'; if it will not be acted on, that is documented too.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Close a finding as FIXED with no link to the PR/commit, merge a change citing a finding without updating its disposition, or create an audit delivery with no disposition section.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Have a verifier judge by a hand-maintained allowlist/exemption set instead of the observed condition — prefer a derived predicate; an unavoidable exemption must be narrow, dated, justified in place, and re-derived.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: A gate declares the surface it covers explicitly; what it does not scan is un-looked-at, not green — a gate covering one surface of a multi-surface rule is either surface-complete or names the un-covered surfaces as out of scope.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: A gate is not 'ready' until it is demonstrated to FAIL on a known-bad input — ship it with a known-bad fixture (negative test) that the gate must reject; a gate with no test or a happy-path-only test is refused.
